title: Implement propagation model
description:
Create python/src/lichen/sim/propagation.py. Log-distance path loss: PL = PL₀ + 10·n·log₁₀(d/d₀). Default PL₀=-127.41dBm at d₀=1m for 915MHz. Configurable exponent n (2.0=free space, 2.7=urban, 3.5=indoor).

SF sensitivity thresholds from LoRaSim (Bor et al., 2016, 'Do LoRa Low-Power Wide-Area Networks Scale?'):
  SF7: -123 dBm, SF8: -126 dBm, SF9: -129 dBm, SF10: -132 dBm, SF11: -134.5 dBm, SF12: -137 dBm
  (at 125kHz BW)

Capture effect threshold: 6 dB (stronger signal wins if delta >= 6dB, per LoRaSim).

We use SF10 fixed, so threshold is -132 dBm.

Class PropagationModel with:
- path_loss(distance_m) -> float (dB)
- snr(tx_power_dbm, distance_m, noise_floor_dbm) -> float
- can_decode(snr) -> bool (snr > sensitivity - noise_floor)

Unit tests with known distances. Cite LoRaSim paper in docstring.
close_reason: Wave 2 complete. 187 tests pass, ruff clean.
